metadata.title: [143] Expose share-link and map-screenshot as plugin API capabilities
metadata.summary: Promotes two capabilities into the public window.mmgisAPI plugin surface: building a share link (writeCoordinateURL, now a pure synchronous builder with the URL-shortener dependency removed) and capturing a map screenshot. Capture becomes engine-aware via a new IMapEngine.captureScreenshot() contract — Leaflet rasterizes its DOM with html2canvas while deck.gl reads its WebGL canvas with preserveDrawingBuffer handling — and BottomBar is refactored into a thin consumer of the same APIs.

overview.purpose: Defines `window.mmgisAPI`, the public JavaScript API that external plugins and embedding applications use to talk to MMGIS. It is the sanctioned boundary between core and everything outside it.
overview.background: The file has two objects: the internal `mmgisAPI_` holds implementations, and the exported `mmgisAPI` maps a curated, JSDoc-documented subset of those onto the public surface. This PR exists so the Share/Export plugin (follow-up PR #168) can get share links and screenshots through this boundary instead of reaching into core internals. For orientation: `window` is the browser's global object, so anything attached to it — like `window.mmgisAPI` — is reachable by any script on the page, including plugins that were never bundled with MMGIS itself. Several methods here return Promises: a Promise is JavaScript's "I'll have the answer later" object, and callers chain `.then(...)` (or `await`) to run code once the value actually arrives.

keyPieces.name: mmgisAPI_
keyPieces.description: Internal implementation object; not exposed directly.

keyPieces.name: mmgisAPI
keyPieces.description: The public surface assigned to `window.mmgisAPI` — each entry carries the JSDoc that serves as the API's documentation.

explanations.title: Track writeCoordinateURL signature change
explanations.what: Drops the `false` argument now that `QueryURL.writeCoordinateURL` no longer takes a leading `shortenURL` flag.
explanations.why: The API always passed `false` (long URL, no shortener call), so behavior for API consumers is unchanged — QueryURL's signature was simplified to be exactly this, and the call site follows.

explanations.title: Add engine-aware getMapScreenshot
explanations.what: New implementation that delegates screenshot capture to the active map engine's `captureScreenshot()` (via `L_.Map_.engine`), rejecting with an error when no engine is loaded.
explanations.why: Capture is fundamentally engine-specific — the Leaflet map is DOM/SVG that html2canvas can rasterize, while the deck.gl map is a WebGL canvas that html2canvas cannot read. Delegating to the `IMapEngine` adapter keeps the API layer free of engine knowledge and means a plugin gets a correct screenshot regardless of which engine the mission is configured with.
explanations.impact: `Map_.engine` is assigned synchronously during map init, so the rejection path only fires before a map exists. Any future engine must implement `captureScreenshot()` (it was added to the IMapEngine contract in this PR).

explanations.title: Publish getMapScreenshot on the API
explanations.what: Exposes `getMapScreenshot` on the public `mmgisAPI` object with JSDoc: async, no backend call, resolves to a PNG data URL, rejects with no active engine.
explanations.why: This is the actual capability grant to plugins — anything not mapped onto `mmgisAPI` is invisible outside core.
explanations.impact: The doc explicitly warns that the deck.gl capture is limited to the GL canvas and omits HTML overlays/markers layered on top — a real behavioral difference from the Leaflet path that consumers need to know about.

overview.purpose: Reads and writes the URL query string that encodes the current view — mission, site, map/globe camera, layer visibility and opacity, active tools, time range — so a view can be shared as a link and restored on load.
overview.background: `writeCoordinateURL` is the write side: it serializes the live app state into a `?mission=...&mapLon=...` string. Before this PR it optionally POSTed that string to a backend URL-shortener service and mutated browser history via a callback. The "query string" is everything after the `?` in a URL (`?mission=...&mapLon=137.4&...`) — a list of key=value pairs the app reads back on page load to rebuild the exact same view. That is the entire sharing mechanism: no server stores the view; the link itself carries it, which is also why links get long.

keyPieces.name: writeCoordinateURL
keyPieces.description: Builds the full share URL from live state; the function this PR reworks.

keyPieces.name: getSingleQueryVariable / grabber functions
keyPieces.description: The read side that restores state from an incoming URL (unchanged).

explanations.title: Make writeCoordinateURL a pure builder
explanations.what: Removes the leading `shortenURL` boolean and the argument-shuffling that let a callback be passed as the first parameter. The function now just takes optional coordinate overrides and returns the URL string synchronously.
explanations.why: As a plugin API capability the share link must work without a backend (static/backend-less deployments) and without hidden side effects. The old signature conflated three jobs — build URL, call the shortener, update browser history — and the callback overloading made the first positional argument ambiguous.
explanations.impact: This is a breaking signature change for any external caller that passed the old boolean first argument — it would now be interpreted as `mapLon`. Both in-repo callers (mmgisAPI and BottomBar) were updated in this PR.

explanations.title: Guard getPanelPercents for modern layout
explanations.what: Checks that `L_.UserInterface_.getPanelPercents` exists before calling it, falling back to a map-only split (`{viewer: 0, map: 100, globe: 0}`).
explanations.why: Only the classic/mobile UI controllers implement `getPanelPercents`; the modern layout has no such method, so building a share link there previously threw. The fallback encodes the modern layout's reality — one full-size map pane.

explanations.title: Drop the URL-shortener side effect
explanations.what: The block that POSTed to `api/shortener/shorten`, rewrote `L_.url`, and called `window.history.replaceState` is gone; the function now ends by returning the long URL.
explanations.why: Shortening required a backend round-trip and a database write, which a pure client-side share capability can't depend on. Callers that want the history update (BottomBar) now do it themselves.
explanations.impact: New share links are always long-form. `shortener_expand` is kept elsewhere, so previously created short links still resolve.

overview.purpose: The registry of named backend API calls used throughout the frontend via `calls.api(name, data, success, error)` — each entry maps a call name to an HTTP method and URL.
overview.background: Think of this file as a phone book: UI code asks for a call by name (`calls.api('shortener_shorten', ...)`) and this registry says which HTTP method and URL that name maps to. Removing an entry is how a frontend feature's backend dependency is formally severed — and because a parity test counts these entries, deleting one also shows up in a test file in this same PR.

explanations.title: Remove shortener_shorten entry
explanations.what: Deletes the `shortener_shorten` registry entry (POST `api/shortener/shorten`); `shortener_expand` stays.
explanations.why: With shortening removed from `writeCoordinateURL`, no code path calls it anymore. Keeping `shortener_expand` means links shortened before this change still expand on load.
explanations.impact: Registry drops from 40 to 39 entries, which the staticHandlers parity test asserts.

overview.purpose: Client-side handlers for static (backend-less) builds: when there is no Node server, `calls.api()` dispatches here instead of making HTTP requests. Every named call in the calls.js registry must have a handler — baked into the bundle, computed client-side, or dropped.
overview.background: A "static build" is MMGIS exported as plain files (HTML/JS/CSS) served with no backend at all — for example straight off S3. Since there is no server to answer API calls, this file provides stand-in implementations that run entirely in the browser. Every named call in calls.js must have a twin here, so the two files change in lockstep.

explanations.title: Drop shortener_shorten handler
explanations.what: Removes the `shortener_shorten: drop()` entry, keeping this table 1:1 with the calls.js registry.
explanations.why: The corresponding calls.js entry was deleted; a stale handler here would fail the parity test that enforces the 1:1 mapping.

overview.purpose: The TypeScript contract every map engine adapter implements (Leaflet, deck.gl). Core code like `Map_` talks to this interface so it stays engine-agnostic; the generic parameters cover each engine's native map, layer, and event types.
overview.background: A TypeScript interface is a contract, not runnable code: it lists the methods an implementation must provide, and the compiler enforces it. Core code is written against this contract so it never needs to know which engine (Leaflet or deck.gl) is actually running — same reason wall sockets have a standard shape. Adding `captureScreenshot` here obligates every engine adapter to supply one.

files.oldContent:
import { LatLng, LatLngLike, PointLike, BoundsLike } from './types/geometry'
import {
    ViewState,
    ViewOptions,
    FlyToOptions,
    FitBoundsOptions,
    MapInitOptions,
} from './types/view'
import { LayerOptions, OverlayOptions } from './types/layers'
import {
    MapEventHandler,
    MapEventOptions,
    FeatureInteractionHandler,
    FeaturePickResult,
    QueryFeaturesOptions,
    DrawShape,
    DrawingOptions,
} from './types/events'
import { MapEngineType } from './types/engine'

/**
 * Core map engine contract.
 *
 * Every adapter (Leaflet, deck.gl) implements this.
 * The interface is intentionally imperative for Leaflet compatibility.
 * The deck.gl adapter translates imperative calls into declarative
 * layer array updates internally.
 *
 * Coordinate convention: all methods accept LatLngLike which is an object
 * with named lat/lng fields. Adapters convert to the engine's native
 * coordinate order internally (Leaflet: [lat, lng], deck.gl: [lng, lat]).
 *
 * TNativeMap is the underlying map (L.Map, Deck, etc).
 * TLayer is the engine's layer type.
 * TEvent is the engine's event object.
 */
export interface IMapEngine<
    TNativeMap = unknown,
    TLayer = unknown,
    TEvent = unknown,
> {
    /**
     * Which engine this adapter represents.
     */
    readonly engineType: MapEngineType

    /**
     * Create the map inside the given container.
     * deck.gl may initialize asynchronously so this can return a promise.
     */
    init(options: MapInitOptions): void | Promise<void>

    /**
     * Tear down the map and free all resources.
     */
    destroy(): void | Promise<void>

    /**
     * Get the underlying engine map object for escape hatch usage.
     */
    getNativeMap(): TNativeMap

    /**
     * Get the DOM container element holding the map.
     */
    getContainer(): HTMLElement

    /**
     * Jump to a center and zoom without animation.
     */
    setView(center: LatLngLike, zoom?: number, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Set zoom without changing center.
     */
    setZoom(zoom: number, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Set center without changing zoom.
     */
    setCenter(center: LatLngLike, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Get the current zoom level.
     */
    getZoom(): number

    /**
     * Get the configured minimum zoom.
     */
    getMinZoom(): number

    /**
     * Get the configured maximum zoom.
     */
    getMaxZoom(): number

    /**
     * Get the current map center as an object with named fields.
     */
    getCenter(): LatLng

    /**
     * Get the visible geographic bounds.
     */
    getBounds(): BoundsLike

    /**
     * Get the full camera state including bearing and pitch.
     */
    getViewState(): ViewState

    /**
     * Set the full camera state. Leaflet adapter ignores bearing and pitch.
     */
    setViewState(state: ViewState, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Set or clear maximum bounds. Pass null to remove the constraint.
     */
    setMaxBounds(bounds: BoundsLike | null): void

    /**
     * Adjust the view so the given bounds fit in the viewport.
     * All engines support this natively. deck.gl adapters use
     * WebMercatorViewport.fitBounds internally.
     */
    fitBounds(bounds: BoundsLike, options?: FitBoundsOptions): void

    /**
     * Smoothly animate to a destination.
     * Leaflet adapter ignores bearing/pitch.
     */
    flyTo(options: FlyToOptions): void

    /**
     * Pan to a new center with animation.
     */
    panTo(center: LatLngLike, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Tell the map its container changed size.
     * Leaflet: invalidateSize(). deck.gl: handled automatically
     * but adapters can force a redraw.
     */
    invalidateSize(): void

    /**
     * Get the pixel size of the map container as [width, height].
     */
    getSize(): PointLike

    /**
     * Get all layers currently on the map.
     */
    getLayers(): TLayer[]

    /**
     * Check if a layer is on the map by layer object or string id.
     */
    hasLayer(layer: TLayer | string): boolean

    /**
     * Add an already created native layer to the map.
     */
    addLayer(layer: TLayer): void

    /**
     * Create a layer from an options spec and add it to the map.
     * deck.gl adapters append to the internal layers array.
     */
    createLayer(options: LayerOptions): TLayer

    /**
     * Remove a layer from the map by layer object or string id.
     */
    removeLayer(layer: TLayer | string): void

    /**
     * Update properties on an existing layer (opacity, style, etc).
     */
    updateLayer(layer: TLayer | string, options: Partial<LayerOptions>): TLayer

    /**
     * Set the z index of a layer to control draw order.
     */
    setLayerZIndex(layer: TLayer | string, zIndex: number): void

    /**
     * Move a layer to the top of the stack.
     */
    bringToFront(layer: TLayer | string): void

    /**
     * Move a layer to the bottom of the stack.
     */
    bringToBack(layer: TLayer | string): void

    /**
     * Set the opacity of a layer.
     */
    setLayerOpacity(layer: TLayer | string, opacity: number): void

    /**
     * Subscribe to a map event (click, moveend, zoomend, etc).
     */
    on(
        eventName: string,
        handler: MapEventHandler<TEvent>,
        options?: MapEventOptions
    ): void

    /**
     * Unsubscribe from a map event.
     */
    off(eventName: string, handler?: MapEventHandler<TEvent>): void

    /**
     * Fire a custom event on the map (e.g. newActiveFeature).
     */
    emit(eventName: string, data?: unknown): void

    /**
     * Register a handler called when the user clicks a feature. Returns an
     * unsubscribe function — call it to detach the handler.
     *
     * Replace semantics: only one handler is active at a time. Calling again
     * with a new handler replaces the previous one; the previous unsubscribe
     * becomes a no-op.
     *
     * Leaflet: wired through map click + leaflet-pip picking.
     * deck.gl: uses onClick picking callback.
     */
    onFeatureClick(handler: FeatureInteractionHandler): () => void

    /**
     * Register a handler called when the user hovers over a feature. Returns
     * an unsubscribe function. See {@link onFeatureClick} for replace
     * semantics.
     *
     * Leaflet: mousemove/mouseout on the map.
     * deck.gl: onHover picking callback.
     */
    onFeatureHover(handler: FeatureInteractionHandler): () => void

    /**
     * Query visible features at a point or within a box.
     * Leaflet: iterate visible layers and test intersection.
     * deck.gl: pickObject / pickObjects.
     */
    queryRenderedFeatures(
        geometry: PointLike | [PointLike, PointLike],
        options?: QueryFeaturesOptions
    ): FeaturePickResult[]

    /**
     * Convert geographic coordinates to pixel coordinates.
     */
    projectCoordinates(latLng: LatLngLike, zoom?: number): PointLike

    /**
     * Convert pixel coordinates to geographic coordinates.
     */
    unprojectCoordinates(point: PointLike, zoom?: number): LatLngLike

    /**
     * Convert container pixel position to geographic coordinates.
     */
    containerPointToLatLng(point: PointLike): LatLngLike

    /**
     * Convert geographic coordinates to container pixel position.
     */
    latLngToContainerPoint(latLng: LatLngLike): PointLike

    /**
     * Begin an interactive drawing session.
     *
     * Polygon: click each vertex; double-click (or `enter`) finishes.
     * Rectangle: two clicks define opposite corners.
     * Circle: first click sets center, second click sets radius. The
     *   completed feature is a 32-segment Polygon approximation.
     *
     * Calling `enableDrawing` while a session is already active first
     * disables the prior session (emitting `drawcancel`), then starts a new
     * one — there is never more than one drawing session at a time on an
     * engine.
     *
     * Engines emit four lifecycle events through the existing `on(name, …)`:
     *   - `drawstart`    payload: {@link DrawStartEvent}
     *   - `drawvertex`   payload: {@link DrawVertexEvent} (committed vertices only)
     *   - `drawcomplete` payload: {@link DrawCompleteEvent}
     *   - `drawcancel`   payload: {@link DrawCancelEvent}
     */
    enableDrawing(shape: DrawShape, options?: DrawingOptions): void

    /**
     * End the active drawing session, removing any in-progress preview
     * geometry from the map. If a session was active, emits `drawcancel`.
     * No-op if no session is active.
     */
    disableDrawing(): void

    /**
     * Commit the current in-progress drawing as a Feature.
     *
     * Emits `drawcomplete` when the current vertices form a valid shape and
     * `drawcancel` when they do not (e.g. polygon with fewer than 3 vertices).
     * Either way the session ends; isDrawing() returns false afterward.
     *
     * This is what plugin "Confirm" buttons should call. Adapters that auto-
     * finish on a built-in interaction (e.g. polygon double-click) call this
     * internally too — there is one finalisation path.
     */
    finishDrawing(): void

    /**
     * Whether a drawing session is currently active.
     */
    isDrawing(): boolean

    /**
     * Attach an HTML overlay anchored to a geographic point.
     *
     * The engine creates a DOM node, appends it to its container, calls
     * `mount(node)` to let the caller render content into it, and keeps it
     * positioned across map view changes. If `mount` returns a function, the
     * engine calls it on `removeOverlay` / engine destroy.
     *
     * Calling `addOverlay` with an `id` that already exists removes the
     * prior overlay first.
     */
    addOverlay(options: OverlayOptions): void

    /**
     * Remove an overlay by id. Runs the cleanup returned by `mount` and
     * removes the DOM node from the container. No-op if the id is unknown.
     */
    removeOverlay(id: string): void
}
files.newContent:
import { LatLng, LatLngLike, PointLike, BoundsLike } from './types/geometry'
import {
    ViewState,
    ViewOptions,
    FlyToOptions,
    FitBoundsOptions,
    MapInitOptions,
} from './types/view'
import { LayerOptions, OverlayOptions } from './types/layers'
import {
    MapEventHandler,
    MapEventOptions,
    FeatureInteractionHandler,
    FeaturePickResult,
    QueryFeaturesOptions,
    DrawShape,
    DrawingOptions,
} from './types/events'
import { MapEngineType } from './types/engine'

/**
 * Core map engine contract.
 *
 * Every adapter (Leaflet, deck.gl) implements this.
 * The interface is intentionally imperative for Leaflet compatibility.
 * The deck.gl adapter translates imperative calls into declarative
 * layer array updates internally.
 *
 * Coordinate convention: all methods accept LatLngLike which is an object
 * with named lat/lng fields. Adapters convert to the engine's native
 * coordinate order internally (Leaflet: [lat, lng], deck.gl: [lng, lat]).
 *
 * TNativeMap is the underlying map (L.Map, Deck, etc).
 * TLayer is the engine's layer type.
 * TEvent is the engine's event object.
 */
export interface IMapEngine<
    TNativeMap = unknown,
    TLayer = unknown,
    TEvent = unknown,
> {
    /**
     * Which engine this adapter represents.
     */
    readonly engineType: MapEngineType

    /**
     * Create the map inside the given container.
     * deck.gl may initialize asynchronously so this can return a promise.
     */
    init(options: MapInitOptions): void | Promise<void>

    /**
     * Tear down the map and free all resources.
     */
    destroy(): void | Promise<void>

    /**
     * Get the underlying engine map object for escape hatch usage.
     */
    getNativeMap(): TNativeMap

    /**
     * Get the DOM container element holding the map.
     */
    getContainer(): HTMLElement

    /**
     * Capture the current map view as a PNG image.
     *
     * Each engine owns the capture strategy for its rendering technology:
     * - Leaflet rasterizes its DOM/SVG/tile panes with html2canvas.
     * - deck.gl reads the WebGL canvas directly (the base map's GL context
     *   when running in interleaved overlay mode), which html2canvas cannot do.
     *
     * @returns Resolves to a PNG image as a data URL string
     *   (e.g. `'data:image/png;base64,...'`).
     */
    captureScreenshot(): Promise<string>

    /**
     * Jump to a center and zoom without animation.
     */
    setView(center: LatLngLike, zoom?: number, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Set zoom without changing center.
     */
    setZoom(zoom: number, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Set center without changing zoom.
     */
    setCenter(center: LatLngLike, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Get the current zoom level.
     */
    getZoom(): number

    /**
     * Get the configured minimum zoom.
     */
    getMinZoom(): number

    /**
     * Get the configured maximum zoom.
     */
    getMaxZoom(): number

    /**
     * Get the current map center as an object with named fields.
     */
    getCenter(): LatLng

    /**
     * Get the visible geographic bounds.
     */
    getBounds(): BoundsLike

    /**
     * Get the full camera state including bearing and pitch.
     */
    getViewState(): ViewState

    /**
     * Set the full camera state. Leaflet adapter ignores bearing and pitch.
     */
    setViewState(state: ViewState, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Set or clear maximum bounds. Pass null to remove the constraint.
     */
    setMaxBounds(bounds: BoundsLike | null): void

    /**
     * Adjust the view so the given bounds fit in the viewport.
     * All engines support this natively. deck.gl adapters use
     * WebMercatorViewport.fitBounds internally.
     */
    fitBounds(bounds: BoundsLike, options?: FitBoundsOptions): void

    /**
     * Smoothly animate to a destination.
     * Leaflet adapter ignores bearing/pitch.
     */
    flyTo(options: FlyToOptions): void

    /**
     * Pan to a new center with animation.
     */
    panTo(center: LatLngLike, options?: ViewOptions): void

    /**
     * Tell the map its container changed size.
     * Leaflet: invalidateSize(). deck.gl: handled automatically
     * but adapters can force a redraw.
     */
    invalidateSize(): void

    /**
     * Get the pixel size of the map container as [width, height].
     */
    getSize(): PointLike

    /**
     * Get all layers currently on the map.
     */
    getLayers(): TLayer[]

    /**
     * Check if a layer is on the map by layer object or string id.
     */
    hasLayer(layer: TLayer | string): boolean

    /**
     * Add an already created native layer to the map.
     */
    addLayer(layer: TLayer): void

    /**
     * Create a layer from an options spec and add it to the map.
     * deck.gl adapters append to the internal layers array.
     */
    createLayer(options: LayerOptions): TLayer

    /**
     * Remove a layer from the map by layer object or string id.
     */
    removeLayer(layer: TLayer | string): void

    /**
     * Update properties on an existing layer (opacity, style, etc).
     */
    updateLayer(layer: TLayer | string, options: Partial<LayerOptions>): TLayer

    /**
     * Set the z index of a layer to control draw order.
     */
    setLayerZIndex(layer: TLayer | string, zIndex: number): void

    /**
     * Move a layer to the top of the stack.
     */
    bringToFront(layer: TLayer | string): void

    /**
     * Move a layer to the bottom of the stack.
     */
    bringToBack(layer: TLayer | string): void

    /**
     * Set the opacity of a layer.
     */
    setLayerOpacity(layer: TLayer | string, opacity: number): void

    /**
     * Subscribe to a map event (click, moveend, zoomend, etc).
     */
    on(
        eventName: string,
        handler: MapEventHandler<TEvent>,
        options?: MapEventOptions
    ): void

    /**
     * Unsubscribe from a map event.
     */
    off(eventName: string, handler?: MapEventHandler<TEvent>): void

    /**
     * Fire a custom event on the map (e.g. newActiveFeature).
     */
    emit(eventName: string, data?: unknown): void

    /**
     * Register a handler called when the user clicks a feature. Returns an
     * unsubscribe function — call it to detach the handler.
     *
     * Replace semantics: only one handler is active at a time. Calling again
     * with a new handler replaces the previous one; the previous unsubscribe
     * becomes a no-op.
     *
     * Leaflet: wired through map click + leaflet-pip picking.
     * deck.gl: uses onClick picking callback.
     */
    onFeatureClick(handler: FeatureInteractionHandler): () => void

    /**
     * Register a handler called when the user hovers over a feature. Returns
     * an unsubscribe function. See {@link onFeatureClick} for replace
     * semantics.
     *
     * Leaflet: mousemove/mouseout on the map.
     * deck.gl: onHover picking callback.
     */
    onFeatureHover(handler: FeatureInteractionHandler): () => void

    /**
     * Query visible features at a point or within a box.
     * Leaflet: iterate visible layers and test intersection.
     * deck.gl: pickObject / pickObjects.
     */
    queryRenderedFeatures(
        geometry: PointLike | [PointLike, PointLike],
        options?: QueryFeaturesOptions
    ): FeaturePickResult[]

    /**
     * Convert geographic coordinates to pixel coordinates.
     */
    projectCoordinates(latLng: LatLngLike, zoom?: number): PointLike

    /**
     * Convert pixel coordinates to geographic coordinates.
     */
    unprojectCoordinates(point: PointLike, zoom?: number): LatLngLike

    /**
     * Convert container pixel position to geographic coordinates.
     */
    containerPointToLatLng(point: PointLike): LatLngLike

    /**
     * Convert geographic coordinates to container pixel position.
     */
    latLngToContainerPoint(latLng: LatLngLike): PointLike

    /**
     * Begin an interactive drawing session.
     *
     * Polygon: click each vertex; double-click (or `enter`) finishes.
     * Rectangle: two clicks define opposite corners.
     * Circle: first click sets center, second click sets radius. The
     *   completed feature is a 32-segment Polygon approximation.
     *
     * Calling `enableDrawing` while a session is already active first
     * disables the prior session (emitting `drawcancel`), then starts a new
     * one — there is never more than one drawing session at a time on an
     * engine.
     *
     * Engines emit four lifecycle events through the existing `on(name, …)`:
     *   - `drawstart`    payload: {@link DrawStartEvent}
     *   - `drawvertex`   payload: {@link DrawVertexEvent} (committed vertices only)
     *   - `drawcomplete` payload: {@link DrawCompleteEvent}
     *   - `drawcancel`   payload: {@link DrawCancelEvent}
     */
    enableDrawing(shape: DrawShape, options?: DrawingOptions): void

    /**
     * End the active drawing session, removing any in-progress preview
     * geometry from the map. If a session was active, emits `drawcancel`.
     * No-op if no session is active.
     */
    disableDrawing(): void

    /**
     * Commit the current in-progress drawing as a Feature.
     *
     * Emits `drawcomplete` when the current vertices form a valid shape and
     * `drawcancel` when they do not (e.g. polygon with fewer than 3 vertices).
     * Either way the session ends; isDrawing() returns false afterward.
     *
     * This is what plugin "Confirm" buttons should call. Adapters that auto-
     * finish on a built-in interaction (e.g. polygon double-click) call this
     * internally too — there is one finalisation path.
     */
    finishDrawing(): void

    /**
     * Whether a drawing session is currently active.
     */
    isDrawing(): boolean

    /**
     * Attach an HTML overlay anchored to a geographic point.
     *
     * The engine creates a DOM node, appends it to its container, calls
     * `mount(node)` to let the caller render content into it, and keeps it
     * positioned across map view changes. If `mount` returns a function, the
     * engine calls it on `removeOverlay` / engine destroy.
     *
     * Calling `addOverlay` with an `id` that already exists removes the
     * prior overlay first.
     */
    addOverlay(options: OverlayOptions): void

    /**
     * Remove an overlay by id. Runs the cleanup returned by `mount` and
     * removes the DOM node from the container. No-op if the id is unknown.
     */
    removeOverlay(id: string): void
}

explanations.title: Add captureScreenshot to the contract
explanations.what: New required method: `captureScreenshot(): Promise<string>`, resolving to a PNG data URL. The doc comment spells out that each engine owns the strategy for its rendering technology.
explanations.why: A single core screenshot routine can't serve both engines — html2canvas rasterizes Leaflet's DOM but cannot read a WebGL canvas, and canvas readback is meaningless for Leaflet's tile/SVG panes. Putting capture behind the engine interface is what lets `mmgisAPI.getMapScreenshot()` stay engine-blind.
explanations.impact: It's a required (not optional) member, so any future adapter must implement it or fail to compile.

overview.purpose: The Leaflet engine's screenshot strategy, extracted nearly verbatim from BottomBar's screenshot button handler into the engine layer. Rasterizes the live map DOM to a PNG data URL with html2canvas after prepping the DOM.
overview.background: html2canvas clones the document and re-renders it onto a canvas. Leaflet's rendering quirks — fractional tile-pane z-indices and absolutely-positioned zoom-animated SVG panes — don't survive that cloning unmodified, hence the pre-capture normalization and the `onclone` fixups. The function takes injectable `jquery`/`html2canvas` deps purely so tests can drive it without a real DOM. Two terms worth knowing: a "data URL" is an entire image encoded as text inside a URL string (`data:image/png;base64,...`), handy because it can be passed around, downloaded, or embedded in a PDF without touching a server. And jQuery (the `$` import) is the pre-React DOM library MMGIS's classic UI is built on — `$('#map')` finds page elements, `.css(...)` mutates their styles in place.

keyPieces.name: getMapScreenshot
keyPieces.description: The single export (default and named); returns Promise<PNG data URL>.

explanations.title: Pre-capture DOM prep and chrome hiding
explanations.what: Normalizes tile-pane z-indices to sequential integers, hides map chrome (scale factor, compass, zoom controls, toolbar offsets), and collapses the time UI — recording the previous state of each so it can be restored.
explanations.why: html2canvas respects z-index but mishandles Leaflet's stacking without the renumber, and UI chrome doesn't belong in an exported map image. New relative to the BottomBar original: the time UI's open state is remembered (`timeUIWasActive`) so it can be reopened — the old code collapsed it and never restored it.

explanations.title: Capture with onclone fixups; modern-layout fallback
explanations.what: Runs html2canvas on `#mapScreen`, falling back to `#map` when the classic wrapper doesn't exist (modern layout). The `onclone` callback re-parents each zoom-animated SVG into a positioned wrapper div and copies live tile-layer z-index styles onto the clone.
explanations.why: The clone renders SVG overlay panes shifted and tile layers mis-stacked without these fixups; they were carried over unchanged from the battle-tested BottomBar code (the comment marks them as must-not-alter). The container fallback is what makes screenshots work at all in the modern layout.

explanations.title: Immediate chrome restore, two bugs fixed
explanations.what: Restores z-indices, chrome visibility, the toolbar offset, and the time UI immediately after the html2canvas call rather than in `.then()`.
explanations.why: html2canvas clones the DOM synchronously before its first internal await, so the capture already holds the hidden-chrome state — restoring right away shortens the visible UI flicker. Two fixes over the BottomBar original: the toolbar restore passes the saved variable (the old code passed the literal string `'savedMapToolBarBottom'`, leaving the toolbar stuck at `0px`), and the time UI is reopened if it was open.

overview.purpose: The IMapEngine implementation wrapping Leaflet — the 2D engine used by most missions. Translates the engine-agnostic contract (view control, layers, events, drawing) into Leaflet calls.
overview.background: An "adapter" wraps a specific library (here Leaflet, the 2D web-mapping library) so it satisfies the shared IMapEngine contract. The screenshot work itself lives in LeafletScreenshot.js; this class just exposes it as the contract's `captureScreenshot()` method, staying a thin translation layer.

explanations.title: Implement captureScreenshot via helper
explanations.what: Satisfies the new IMapEngine method by delegating to `getMapScreenshot` from the sibling LeafletScreenshot module (imported at line 49).
explanations.why: The html2canvas/DOM-prep logic is substantial, jQuery-heavy JavaScript; keeping it in its own module keeps this TypeScript adapter thin and lets tests mock the strategy while asserting the adapter delegates to it.

overview.purpose: The IMapEngine implementation for the deck.gl/WebGL engine. Runs in two modes: overlay (deck.gl layers interleaved into a maplibre/mapbox basemap's GL context — the normal modern-map case) or standalone (deck.gl owns the only canvas).
overview.background: The key WebGL constraint driving this diff: by default the GL drawing buffer is cleared after every composite, so `canvas.toDataURL()` returns a blank image unless the context was created with `preserveDrawingBuffer: true` AND the read happens in the same frame as a render. That's why the capture logic forces a repaint and why both GL-context creation sites gain the flag. For orientation: a WebGL canvas is drawn by the GPU rather than composed from HTML elements, which is why DOM-cloning tools like html2canvas see it as a blank rectangle — its pixels must be read back from the GPU's drawing buffer instead, and that buffer is only readable under the conditions this diff arranges.

keyPieces.name: captureScreenshot
keyPieces.description: New: reads the GL canvas back as a PNG data URL, per mode.

keyPieces.name: _initStandaloneMode / _setupOverlay
keyPieces.description: The two GL-context creation paths; both now request preserveDrawingBuffer.

keyPieces.name: BasemapInstance
keyPieces.description: Structural typing for the maplibre/mapbox map object the adapter drives.

explanations.title: Extend BasemapInstance typing
explanations.what: Adds `once`, `getCanvas`, and optional `redraw`/`triggerRepaint`/`loaded` to the structural type for the basemap map object.
explanations.why: `getCanvas`, `redraw` (maplibre-only, synchronous), and `triggerRepaint` (both libs, schedules a frame) are what `captureScreenshot` needs; the optionality encodes the real API difference between maplibre-gl and mapbox-gl.
explanations.impact: `once` and `loaded` are declared but not exercised by the code in this PR.

explanations.title: WebGL screenshot via canvas readback
explanations.what: Implements `captureScreenshot()`. Overlay mode: force a basemap repaint — synchronously via `redraw()` when available, else `triggerRepaint()` plus a requestAnimationFrame — then read `basemap.getCanvas().toDataURL('image/png')`. Standalone mode: `deck.redraw('screenshot')` then read deck's canvas. Rejects when there's no map or canvas.
explanations.why: Reading in the same frame as a render is the only way toDataURL returns pixels (see the preserveDrawingBuffer constraint). Overlay mode reads the basemap's canvas because interleaved deck.gl draws into the base map's GL context — one canvas holds basemap plus deck layers.
explanations.impact: Only the GL canvas is captured: anchored HTML overlays/markers added via addOverlay are separate DOM nodes and won't appear in the screenshot. The rAF fallback path resolves whatever is on the canvas next frame; if the scheduled repaint hasn't completed by then, a stale-but-rendered frame is still readable thanks to preserveDrawingBuffer.

explanations.title: Standalone preserveDrawingBuffer via deviceProps
explanations.what: Standalone `Deck` construction now passes `deviceProps: { webgl: { preserveDrawingBuffer: true } }`.
explanations.why: deck.gl v9 moved GL context options from the v8-era `glOptions` prop to `deviceProps.webgl` (v9 sits on the luma.gl Device abstraction). v9 happens to default this to true, but setting it explicitly means the screenshot guarantee doesn't ride on an upstream default.

explanations.title: Basemap preserveDrawingBuffer
explanations.what: The maplibre/mapbox Map constructor options gain `preserveDrawingBuffer: true`.
explanations.why: The overlay-mode capture reads this canvas; without the flag the export is blank. It must be set at map-creation time — it can't be toggled on later.
explanations.impact: Costs an extra buffer copy per frame for every deck.gl-engine session, screenshot or not. The comment judges that acceptable for a basemap-backed view.

overview.purpose: The modern layout's bottom bar, which hosts the 'Copy Link' and 'Screenshot' buttons. Before this PR it owned the full share-link and screenshot implementations inline; now it is a thin consumer of the same public capabilities plugins get.
overview.background: This file is where the ~90 lines of html2canvas logic (now LeafletScreenshot.js) used to live. The refactor is the proof-of-concept for the PR's premise: if core UI can drive share/screenshot purely through `QueryURL.writeCoordinateURL()` and `mmgisAPI.getMapScreenshot()`, a plugin can too. This is classic jQuery UI code — buttons are built as `$('<i>')` elements and wired with `.on('click', ...)` handlers; no React here. The sync/async split matters in both changes: the link copy now happens immediately inside the click handler, while the screenshot arrives later through a Promise, so its handler chains `.then(...)`.

explanations.title: Share click goes synchronous
explanations.what: The link button now calls `writeCoordinateURL()` directly, updates browser history itself, and copies to the clipboard inline — the success animation no longer waits inside a shortener callback.
explanations.why: `writeCoordinateURL` stopped doing the shortener round-trip and the `history.replaceState`, so the side effects this UI actually wants move here, where they belong.
explanations.impact: Users now always copy the long URL; there is no short-link fallback.

explanations.title: Screenshot delegates to mmgisAPI
explanations.what: The screenshot click handler shrinks to: show spinner, `window.mmgisAPI.getMapScreenshot()`, build the filename from mission/time/center, download the PNG via a blob URL, hide spinner — with a `.catch` that logs and clears the spinner.
explanations.why: The old inline html2canvas code was Leaflet-only and duplicated what is now the engine layer's job; routing through the API makes the button work on the deck.gl engine too. The blob-URL download replaces `F_.downloadCanvas` because large/hi-DPI captures inflate ~33% as base64 and big data-URL anchors are unreliable across browsers.
explanations.impact: The old handler had no error path — a failed capture left the spinner up forever. The new `.catch` fixes that.

overview.purpose: New unit tests pinning `QueryURL.writeCoordinateURL`'s reworked contract: synchronous string return, no backend call, and no throw in the modern layout.
overview.background: L_ and T_ are shared singletons, so the tests snapshot and restore every property they stub. Viewer_ is mocked out because it transitively imports JSX-in-.js viewer files that vitest's import analysis can't parse. A "singleton" is one shared object that every module imports (here `L_`), so a test that mutates it would leak state into the next test — hence the snapshot/restore ritual around each one. "Stubbing" means installing minimal fake values so the code under test can run without the real app behind it.

explanations.title: Minimal singleton stubbing
explanations.what: Stubs just enough of L_ (map center/zoom, mission, layers, config) and T_ for every branch of `writeCoordinateURL` to reach the return, restoring the singletons after each test.
explanations.why: The function reads a wide swath of global app state; stubbing the exact touched surface keeps the test honest about what the function depends on.

explanations.title: Sync contract and modern-layout fallback
explanations.what: Asserts the return is a string (not a Promise) containing the expected mission/coordinate/panePercents params, and that a `UserInterface_` without `getPanelPercents` doesn't throw and yields `panePercents=0,100,0`.
explanations.why: These are the two behavioral commitments of the QueryURL change: purity/synchronicity and the modern-layout guard.

overview.purpose: Parity tests ensuring every entry in the calls.js registry has a matching static-build handler in staticHandlers.js.
overview.background: A parity test doesn't check behavior; it counts and matches names across two files so they can't drift apart. When a registry entry is deleted (as this PR does), the expected count here must drop with it — the test failing until it does is the point.

explanations.title: Registry count 40 → 39
explanations.what: Updates the expected calls.js registry size after `shortener_shorten` was removed.
explanations.why: The count assertion exists precisely to force this conscious update whenever the registry changes; the surrounding 1:1 parity tests are unchanged and still pass.

overview.purpose: New unit tests for `mmgisAPI.getMapScreenshot()`'s delegation contract: call the active engine's `captureScreenshot()`, reject when there is no engine or the engine lacks the method.
overview.background: Drives `L_.Map_.engine` directly (with save/restore around each test) since L_ is a shared singleton; Viewer_ is stubbed to keep the mmgisAPI import chain parseable under vitest.

explanations.title: Delegation and guard-path coverage
explanations.what: Three tests: a fake engine's `captureScreenshot` is called exactly once and its resolved value passed through; a null engine rejects; an engine without the method rejects.
explanations.why: Locks in the 'reject rather than reach into a specific engine's DOM' behavior — the API must never fall back to Leaflet-specific capture when no engine is active.

overview.purpose: New behavior tests for the extracted `LeafletScreenshot.getMapScreenshot()`, exercised through its injectable `jquery`/`html2canvas` dependencies since neither the real DOM nor html2canvas exists in the Node test context.
overview.background: "Dependency injection" here means the function accepts its tools (jquery, html2canvas) as an optional argument, so tests can pass hand-built fakes that record how they were called — no real browser rendering needed. The fakes look elaborate, but each only mimics the tiny slice of jQuery/html2canvas behavior the code under test actually touches.

explanations.title: Recording fakes for jQuery and html2canvas
explanations.what: A fake jQuery records every `.css(prop, value)` set and `.trigger()` call keyed by selector; a fake html2canvas records its element/options and resolves a canned canvas; a minimal global document/window is installed per test.
explanations.why: The function's observable behavior is a sequence of DOM mutations plus one html2canvas call — recording fakes let the tests assert that sequence without a browser.

explanations.title: Export surface and capture basics
explanations.what: Verifies the default and named exports are the same function, the resolved value is html2canvas's PNG data URL, html2canvas runs once on `#mapScreen` with correct window dimensions and an `onclone` option, and each piece of chrome is hidden then restored.
explanations.why: Pins the module's public contract and the hide/restore symmetry that keeps the live UI intact after a capture.

explanations.title: Regression guards for the two carried-over bugs
explanations.what: Asserts `#mapToolBar` bottom is restored to the saved value (`'5px'`) and never to the literal string `'savedMapToolBarBottom'`, and that the time UI is toggled off via the `.active` selector then reopened via the plain selector.
explanations.why: Both were live bugs in the BottomBar original (quoted-variable-name restore; missing time-UI reopen) fixed during the extraction — these tests keep them fixed.

overview.purpose: The existing LeafletAdapter unit suite (lifecycle, view control, layers, events); this PR adds coverage for the new `captureScreenshot` delegation.
overview.background: `vi.mock(path, factory)` is vitest's module swap: any import of that path anywhere in this test file receives the fake instead of the real module. That lets the suite assert the adapter *delegates* to the screenshot helper without ever executing html2canvas.

explanations.title: Mock the Leaflet screenshot strategy
explanations.what: `vi.mock`s the LeafletScreenshot module with a spy resolving a sentinel data URL, imported back as `mockedLeafletCapture` for assertions.
explanations.why: The real helper drives html2canvas and the live DOM; mocking isolates the adapter's one job here — delegation.

explanations.title: Assert real delegation, not existence
explanations.what: Calls `adapter.captureScreenshot()` and asserts the mocked helper was invoked exactly once and its promise value returned.
explanations.why: A method-exists check would pass even if the adapter reimplemented or dropped the call; this pins the delegation itself.

overview.purpose: The existing DeckGLAdapter unit suite; this PR adds a `captureScreenshot` block covering both engine modes and the failure path.
overview.background: These tests hand the adapter fake `_basemap`/`_deck` objects with just enough surface (a canvas with `toDataURL`, a `triggerRepaint`) to walk each capture path — the standard pattern when the real dependency needs a GPU that the test environment doesn't have.

explanations.title: Import MAP_ENGINE from types module
explanations.what: Switches the `MAP_ENGINE` import from `MapEngines/index.ts` to the lightweight `types/engine.ts`.
explanations.why: `index.ts` transitively imports LeafletAdapter → leaflet, which touches a global `window` at module-eval time and crashes this Node test context — an unrelated suite-unblocking fix.

explanations.title: Cover both capture modes and rejection
explanations.what: Four tests using injected fake `_basemap`/`_deck`: overlay mode must redraw before reading (the fake returns BLANK unless redrawn — ordering is asserted, not just calls), the no-`redraw()` path falls back to triggerRepaint + rAF, standalone mode calls `deck.redraw('screenshot')` and reads deck's canvas, and no map rejects.
explanations.why: The redraw-before-read ordering is the crux of the WebGL readback correctness; a test that only checked call counts would miss a blank-screenshot regression.
explanations.impact: These tests bypass `init()` by assigning private fields, so they cannot catch a misconfigured GL context — that's what deckGLScreenshotConfig.spec.js exists for.

overview.purpose: New tests guarding that DeckGLAdapter's real `init()` requests `preserveDrawingBuffer: true` on both GL-context creation paths — the setting the whole deck.gl screenshot feature depends on.
overview.background: The captureScreenshot tests inject fake `_basemap`/`_deck` and never run `init()`, so nothing else verifies the context is created readable. This suite runs the real init with the GL constructors mocked (jsdom has no WebGL). It is specifically a deck.gl v9 shape guard: the flag lives at `deviceProps.webgl.preserveDrawingBuffer` on Deck (the v8 `glOptions` prop is gone) and top-level on the maplibre Map options.

explanations.title: Constructor-recording GL mocks
explanations.what: Mocks `@deck.gl/core` (keeping the real module except a `MockDeck` that records its constructor props) and `maplibre-gl` (a `MockMap` recording its options); DeckGLAdapter is imported after the mocks via top-level await.
explanations.why: Recording constructor arguments is the only way to observe what init() would hand a real GL context without creating one.

explanations.title: Assert the flag at its v9 locations
explanations.what: Standalone init must yield `deckCtorArgs[0].deviceProps.webgl.preserveDrawingBuffer === true`; maplibre overlay init must yield top-level `preserveDrawingBuffer: true` on the Map options.
explanations.why: If a future refactor or deck.gl upgrade moves or drops the flag, screenshots silently become blank images — this converts that into a test failure naming the exact option path.
